title: pittosporum brevicalyx
intro: Pittosporum brevicalyx, also known as the Australian or New Zealand lacebark tree, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and bark of Pittosporum brevicalyx have been used in herbal preparations to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and to promote overall well-being. In addition to its medicinal uses, Pittosporum brevicalyx has been used in traditional herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to support immune function and reduce stress.
